name: Laos

visa_free.notes: ASEAN member state; visa-free for ordinary passport holders. Multiple sources confirm 30 days; earlier sources reporting 14 days appear outdated.

Extended from 15 days to 30 days effective June 1, 2025, announced by Lao President Thongloun Sisoulith during visit to Japan on May 29, 2025 marking 70th anniversary of diplomatic relations.

Visa exemption for ordinary passport holders implemented August 15, 2025. Initial diplomatic/official passport agreement was signed October 8, 2024; expanded to ordinary passports in May 2025 and implemented August 2025.

Mutual visa exemption agreement for ordinary (national) passport holders signed July 17, 2025 in Vientiane. Entered into force January 15, 2026. Stay must not exceed 30 days from entry date; total stay within 90 days per calendar year. Passport must have at least 180 days validity.

visa_on_arrival.notes: Visa on arrival available for most foreign nationals at designated international checkpoints. Extension possible twice for a maximum stay of 90 days. Nationals of the following countries are NOT eligible for VOA and must obtain visa from Lao embassies/consulates abroad: Afghanistan, Algeria, Bangladesh, Burundi, Cameroon, Congo, DR Congo, Eswatini, Ghana, Guinea-Bissau, Iran, Iraq, Ivory Coast, Jordan, Lebanon, Lesotho, Liberia, Libya, Mozambique, Nauru, Niger, Nigeria, Pakistan, Senegal, Sierra Leone, South Sudan, Sudan, Suriname, Syria, Tonga, Zambia, Zimbabwe. This updated list of 32 countries supersedes the original MFA Notice No. 1232 (October 2009) list of 28 countries; additions include DR Congo, Eswatini (formerly Swaziland), Iran, Iraq, Lebanon, South Sudan, Sudan. Exception for those on official visits with MFA guarantee.

e_visa.notes: Ordinary Indian passport holders must apply for a Lao e-Visa via laoevisa.gov.la (Ministry of Foreign Affairs). India is explicitly listed on the official 'Who Can Apply' page. e-Visa valid 60 days from issuance; maximum stay 30 days, single-entry. Fee: USD 50. Processing: 3 business days. Aggregator claims of visa-free or visa-on-arrival for India are FALSE for ordinary passport holders. Diplomatic and service passport holders are visa-free (30 days) under bilateral agreement effective 06.11.2002.

Lao e-Visa (laoevisa.gov.la) is available to approximately 162 nationalities. e-Visa valid for 60 days from date of issuance; allows maximum stay of 30 days. Issued in two categories: Travel Visa (tourism) and Visit Visa (visiting hosts/organizations/meetings). Both are single-entry. Fee: USD 50. Processing: 3 business days standard. Application at official portal laoevisa.gov.la (Ministry of Foreign Affairs). Certain territories/nationalities must obtain sticker visa at Lao embassy instead. Specific port-of-entry restrictions apply. Launched July 2019. Chinese ordinary passport holders are eligible for e-visa. India (IND) is explicitly listed on official eligible-country page.

visa_policy.notes: The official immigration.gov.la website is rendered as a JavaScript SPA; static content is not directly accessible via WebFetch. Visa exemption data is sourced from: (1) immigration.gov.la/en/visas (confirmed official); (2) tourismlaos.org (official Laos Tourism Marketing Department under Ministry of Information, Culture and Tourism); (3) Chinese MFA official bilateral agreements lists (cs.mfa.gov.cn and en.nia.gov.cn); (4) Belarusian MFA official press releases. CORRECTION (2026-06-13): China (CHN) ordinary passport holders are NOT visa-free to Laos — they must obtain a VOA or e-visa. The 1989 China-Laos agreement covers only diplomatic/service/official passports and Lao ordinary passports with valid official visas for public affairs; it does not grant visa-free entry to Chinese ordinary passport holders. China has been removed from visa_free and the conditional_access entry corrected accordingly. VOA restricted list updated from 28 (MFA Notice 1232, 2009) to 32 countries to reflect current 2025 list. Digital Immigration Form (LDIF) required for arrivals at major airports and Lao-Thai Friendship Bridge I effective September 1, 2025.

Bilateral visa exemption agreement effective 06.11.1989 for diplomatic, service, and official (public affairs) passports. Ordinary Chinese passport holders are NOT visa-free — they must obtain a VOA or e-visa to enter Laos. The 1989 agreement also covers Lao ordinary passports with valid official visas for public affairs travel (not relevant to Chinese ordinary passport holders seeking tourist/general entry). The temporary 15-day visa-free entry for Chinese tourists under Visit Laos Year 2024 ended December 31, 2024.

cbi.program_name: Honorary Citizenship by Investment (Decree No. 14 of 2022)

options.notes: Minimum USD 500,000 donation toward socio-economic development of Laos (required in addition to the investment component)
Minimum USD 1,000,000 foreign investment into Laos (required in addition to the donation component). Total minimum commitment: USD 1,500,000.

cbi.processing_time: Ministry of Justice reviews within 5 business days of receipt, then evaluates for 30 days before forwarding to government for further review. Applications submitted to Ministry of Foreign Affairs or Lao consulate/embassy abroad.
cbi.notes: Decree No. 14 issued September 2022, announced October 2022. This is HONORARY citizenship, not full citizenship — it does not confer all rights of ordinary Lao citizens. Honorary citizens receive: visa-free entry/exit of Laos, right to reside permanently, right to purchase state-owned land for specified period, right to lease state and privately-owned land. Cannot own land outright. Applicants must be 18+, have no criminal record for intentional crimes, good health, no illegal drug use/conviction. Professional qualifications at doctorate/professor level preferred. Program has drawn skepticism due to honorary (not full) nature and Lao passport's low global mobility. Operational status as a functioning program in 2026 is uncertain — the program was gazetted but implementation details and whether applications are actively processed remains unclear from official sources only.

programs.name: Investor Visa (NI-B2) / Business Visa for Foreign Investors
programs.notes: Business visa for foreign investors legally approved by the Government of Lao PDR. Requires investment license, concession registration, or enterprise registration. Annual renewals for stay permit cards (3-month, 6-month, or 12-month options at 300,000/600,000/1,200,000 kip respectively). Long-term stay permits (3-5 years) only for concession holders with 10+ year government agreements. Eligible categories: foreign investors, stockholders, directors, deputy directors, technical officers and family members. Managed by the Investment Promotion and Management Committee (IPMC) / investlaos.gov.la.

agreements: ASEAN (bilateral visa-free agreements with all 9 other ASEAN member states for ordinary passport holders)

data_quality.validation_notes: Validated 2026-06-13. Key corrections made vs prior record: (1) REMOVED China (CHN) from visa_policy.visa_free — Chinese ordinary passport holders are NOT visa-free to Laos; both Chinese MFA sources (cs.mfa.gov.cn and en.nia.gov.cn) confirm the 1989 agreement only covers diplomatic/service/official passports; Chinese tourists use VOA or e-visa. (2) CORRECTED China conditional_access entry to reflect passport_types=[diplomatic, service, official] (not ordinary) and clarified the temporary Visit Laos Year 2024 exemption ended December 31, 2024. (3) UPDATED VOA restricted list from 28 countries (MFA Notice No. 1232, 2009) to 32 countries, adding DR Congo, Eswatini (formerly Swaziland), Iran, Iraq, Lebanon, South Sudan, Sudan per current 2025 sources. (4) UPDATED Japan notes to clarify previous stay was 15 days (extended to 30 days June 1, 2025). (5) CONFIRMED Belarus ordinary passport exemption entered force January 15, 2026 per Belarusian MFA. (6) Updated e-visa count from 160+ to 162+ based on updated sources. CORRECTION 2026-06-14: ADDED India (IND) as explicit entry in visa_policy.e_visa — confirmed via official laoevisa.gov.la/article/who_can_apply (Consular Dept, Ministry of Foreign Affairs LAO PDR) which explicitly names India as e-visa eligible; aggregator claim of visa-free or visa-on-arrival for ordinary Indian passport holders is FALSE; also updated gap entry to reflect who_can_apply page is now accessible (was previously returning 404 in prior scrape attempts).
gaps: immigration.gov.la renders as JavaScript SPA — visa exemption table and restricted list embedded in images not accessible via text scraping; official table dated Vientiane 12 October 2022 remains most authoritative accessible source

laoevisa.gov.la/article/who_can_apply page confirmed accessible as of 2026-06-14 via Firecrawl scrape; India is explicitly named in the list; e-visa country count updated to reflect ~162+ eligible nationalities
Exact stay duration for Brunei ordinary passport disputed in older sources (14 vs 30 days); 30 days supported by majority of current sources including ASEAN context
Myanmar stay duration (14 days) sourced from aggregator references to Ministry of Public Security list; official page inaccessible for direct confirmation
CBI (Decree No. 14) operational status unclear — program was gazetted but whether applications are actively accepted and processed in 2026 is unknown from accessible official sources
Dual citizenship policy for honorary citizenship not addressed in Decree No. 14 as accessed
No credential-based conditional entry rules found — Laos does not offer visa-free or VOA to holders of US/Schengen/UK/other visas or residence permits based on that credential alone
No OCI (special status) rules found — OCI cardholders must obtain a regular visa
Timor-Leste ordinary passport exemption source is Laotian Times (citing KPL state news), not directly from immigration.gov.la
VOA updated restricted list (32 countries) sourced from inside-laos.com and Wikipedia cross-reference; official current MFA notice updating Notice No. 1232 not directly accessible

China passport_type conditional_access: the 1989 agreement text mentions 'ordinary passports with effective visas for public affairs of Laos' — this applies to Lao nationals, not Chinese ordinary passport holders seeking entry to Laos

outbound_official.note: no official Laos government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data

Invest Laos - Visa & Stay Permit Card page (official IPMC)

Belarusian MFA - Belarus-Laos visa exemption agreement news (signed July 2025)

Tourism Laos - End of Visit Laos Year 2024 special exemptions (China, HK/Macau/Taiwan temporary exemptions ended December 31, 2024)
Inside Laos - VOA restricted list (32 countries in 2025, updated from original 28-country MFA notice)
Wikipedia - Visa policy of Laos (aggregator; used to cross-check VOA restriction list and diplomatic passport agreement dates, not as primary source)
Laotian Times (leading English news, cites KPL state news agency) - Laos-Timor-Leste visa exemption implementation August 2025
US State Department - Laos international travel information (confirms VOA and e-visa available; no specific exemption nationality list)
